name: Longer-term spermidine trial did not improve primary memory outcome
summary: The best-controlled longer-duration spermidine trial in this track was null on its primary memory endpoint and secondary outcomes.
statement: A 12-month randomized placebo-controlled spermidine trial in 100 older adults with subjective cognitive decline did not significantly improve mnemonic discrimination performance or secondary outcomes versus placebo.
population_or_model: Older adults with subjective cognitive decline
quantitative_note: Primary endpoint between-group difference was -0.03 with 95% CI -0.11 to 0.05 and P = .47.
caveats: The intervention was low-dose spermidine-rich wheat germ extract rather than a general test of all autophagy-inducing strategies.
Exploratory verbal-memory and inflammation signals require validation and do not override the null primary endpoint.
The endpoint was cognitive function in a specific at-risk population, not lifespan or broad healthy-aging function.
